body.title: Audit: find bespoke routing logic that should be rules engines
body.result: audit complete; 28 patterns; 2 refactor tickets filed (T-librarian-tool-dispatcher, T-queue-mcp-dispatcher)
body.description:
The Registered Dispatcher pattern (C-registered-dispatcher, §3.10 TheoryOfOperation) is the canonical shape for: queue of work + self-registering handler registry + routing rules. As the codebase grows, bespoke if/elif routing chains accumulate that should instead be rules engines. This ticket is a one-time audit sweep: grep for routing patterns, classify each as appropriate-bespoke or should-be-rules-engine, and file follow-on tickets for the refactor candidates.

**Affected files:** Output: lab/design_docs/decisions/D-rules-engine-audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md + follow-on tickets
**Design rules:** none apply — audit and ticket-filing only, no code changes in this ticket
**Scope boundary:** In scope: grep devices/ + unseen_university/ for if/elif dispatch chains >3 branches, evaluate against C-registered-dispatcher criteria, produce report + tickets. Out of scope: performing the refactors (each gets its own ticket).
**Completion criteria:** (1) audit report written listing each candidate with verdict (refactor / leave / monitor); (2) tickets filed for any refactor-now candidates; (3) report committed to lab/design_docs/decisions/
